notes.note: The Luperci were the priests who ran the wild Lupercalia rite each February; young Quintus had joined them, very likely the new Caesarian college (the Luperci Iulii). Cicero counts the appointment, a Caesarian favour, as a disgrace to the family.

The Greek word Cicero uses, eranos, denotes a friendly whip-round or interest-free loan among friends. He is indignant that his brother had the cheek to ask Atticus to chip in for young Quintus's affairs.

notes.anchor: his own Cato pleases Bassus Lucilius too
notes.note: A barbed comparison: Cicero's Cato delights him, but a man named Lucilius Bassus is equally pleased by his own (inferior) Cato. The Bassus meant has no secure identification.
